title: EV maker Lucid suspends production guidance amid incoming CEO's business review
text: Lucid Group suspended vehicle production guidance for the year as its incoming CEO evaluates the company's business operations. While Lucid had previously said in February 2026 that it expected to build between 25,000 and 27,000 vehicles in 2026, the company suspended this annual guidance pending the CEO's review. The suspension came after disappointing Q1 2026 results and concerns about production feasibility given recent supply chain issues. Incoming CEO Silvio Napoli was announced to take over from interim CEO Marc Winterhoff. The guidance suspension reflects management's desire to conduct a comprehensive business review before committing to updated production targets. This move signaled potential downward revisions to output expectations and highlighted ongoing challenges with production quality and delivery consistency.
